Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for Liam Park or StormCloud using search_emails (query: "Liam Park" or "StormCloud"). Find Liam's April 24 escalation email listing four unresolved issues: Jae-won Kim housing, $500 goodwill credit, Simone Richter stipend, and Ridgeline Robotics referral. Also find Mina's recovery plan response to Liam (same day) with specific commitments on each item.
Search emails for the Jae-won Kim housing thread using search_emails (query: "Jae-won" or "guarantor" or "UrbanNest Austin"). Find Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest flagging on April 8 that the lease at Barton Creek Flats requires a corporate guarantor packet. Find Suki Patel's April 9 reply promising to send the packet by end of week. Find no evidence the packet was actually sent. → Read action. Status: guarantor packet NOT sent.
Search Airtable for Jae-won Kim's relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field: Name, value: "Jae-won Kim"). Find record recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin. The Status field reads "In Progress" (modified April 24 after the reconstruction), with Special Requirements noting "Lease still unsigned — UrbanNest flagged landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork." → Read action. Confirms housing is NOT finalized.
Search Linear for StormCloud issues using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud"). Find ticket #284 (linear_issue_a49b4d199139): "StormCloud account incident reconstruction — Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop", priority P1, assigned to Mina. Read comments using linear_list_comments to find: Chloe's ops review confirming the guarantor packet was never sent, Marcus confirming the $500 credit misposting, Julian providing support context on the original flight recovery, and David admitting the Ridgeline referral was never actioned until April 24. → Read action. Master reconstruction ticket already exists.
Search emails or Slack for the $500 goodwill credit trail using search_emails (query: "$500 credit" or "goodwill" or "EST-2026-0287"). Find Marcus Thorne's confirmation that the $500 credit was applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319. Find Julian Brooks' support context confirming the original credit commitment was legitimate. → Read action. Status: credit NOT on the correct invoice.
Search QuickBooks for StormCloud invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: CustomerRef "StormCloud" or similar). Find invoice inv_stormcloud_01 (INV-2026-0319) showing $26,400 balance for 3 relocations with no $500 credit applied. → Read action. Confirms credit was never moved to the live invoice.
Search emails for Simone Richter's stipend using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "standing desk" or "reimbursement"). Find Simone's April 16 email asking about her $340 standing desk claim (originally submitted March 25) still in Pending Review. Find Mina's forward to Marcus asking to release it. Find Marcus's refusal: "We are not releasing any pending stipend reimbursements at this time" due to the blanket fraud review freeze. → Read action. Status: $340 still frozen, no exception granted.
Search emails for the Ridgeline Robotics referral using search_emails (query: "Ridgeline" or "Kenji Watanabe" or "Anya Petrova"). Find Anya Petrova's April 18 warm introduction email to David and Mina, CC'ing Kenji. Find Kenji's April 21 follow-up asking to schedule a call (went unanswered). Find David's late April 24 reply to Kenji offering call times. Find Mina's April 24 reply to Kenji apologizing for the delay. → Read action. Status: recovering, replies sent April 24, awaiting Kenji's response.
Search CRM for Ridgeline Robotics using crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline") or crm_search_leads (full_name: "Kenji Watanabe"). Find company record crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2, lead crm_lead_de04045463c3, and deal crm_deal_a95b4721982a ($8,750, Qualified To Buy). David created these on April 24. → Read action. CRM entries exist for Ridgeline.
Search CRM for StormCloud deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "StormCloud") or crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_stormcloud"). Find deal_stormcloud ($25,650, proposalMade) and deal_stormcloud_won ($26,400, closedWon). Neither deal reflects the current account risk or Liam's escalation. → Read action. CRM deal stage does not match reality.
Look up contact details for Liam Park using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Liam Park"). Find liam.park@stormcloud.io. Look up David Chen's email. Find david.chen@moveops.com. → Read action (default tool, contacts lookup).
Update CRM deal stage for StormCloud or create a new deal to reflect current account risk using crm_update_deal_stage or crm_create_deal. The existing closedWon deal does not capture the April escalation or churn risk. → Write action.
Log a detailed CRM engagement note using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: NOTE, company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]) covering verification findings on all four items: Jae-won housing (guarantor not sent, still in temp), $500 credit (on wrong estimate, not corrected), Simone stipend (frozen under blanket hold), Ridgeline (replies sent April 24, awaiting response). → Write action.
Create or update Linear tickets for unresolved items using linear_create_issue or linear_update_issue. Items still needing resolution: (1) Jae-won guarantor packet must be sent to UrbanNest, (2) $500 credit must be moved from EST-2026-0287 to INV-2026-0319, (3) Simone's $340 stipend needs a release decision. Each ticket needs a clear owner and deadline. → Write action.
Send email to Liam Park using send_email (from: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, to: liam.park@stormcloud.io) with an honest per-item status update: what has been verified as completed, what is still in progress, and specific timelines for remaining items. → Write action.
Send email to David Chen using send_email (from: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, to: david.chen@moveops.com) with an internal briefing covering all four recovery items, verification findings, CRM/Linear changes made, and what David needs to be aware of before Monday. → Write action.
Post a message in #customer-engagement using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C002) flagging the StormCloud account status so the team has visibility on the recovery verification and next steps. → Write action.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Rubric Results

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 An email was sent (via send_email) from [mina.hashimoto@moveops.com](mailto:mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) to [liam.park@stormcloud.io](mailto:liam.park@stormcloud.io).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Call #93 in the trajectory is a send_email with sender mina.hashimoto@moveops.com and recipient liam.park@stormcloud.io. The call succeeded.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows a send_email call with sender 'mina.hashimoto@moveops.com' and recipients ['liam.park@stormcloud.io'], which returned a successful email_id (502a4afca0e24adb83d98fc12395aae9).
✓Run #3A send_email tool call was made with sender 'mina.hashimoto@moveops.com' and recipients ['liam.park@stormcloud.io']. The call succeeded with email_id 'a1f41020a3ed453184cd457cfcb18311'.

✓Run #6The trajectory shows a send_email tool call with sender 'mina.hashimoto@moveops.com' and recipients ['liam.park@stormcloud.io'], which succeeded and returned email_id '815e836d42c2400997840af0d5e6e200'.
2 The email to [liam.park@stormcloud.io](mailto:liam.park@stormcloud.io) mentions the Jae-won Kim housing issue (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to liam.park@stormcloud.io explicitly covers Jae-won Kim's housing issue as item #1, noting the guarantor packet was promised by 4/24 but actually sent 4/26 and is awaiting lease countersignature.
✓Run #2The email to Liam contains a clearly labeled section '1. JAE-WON KIM — HOUSING' and describes the guarantor packet failure and housing status in detail.
✓Run #3The email to Liam contains a section explicitly titled '1. Jae-won Kim's housing — IN PROGRESS, NOT YET RESOLVED' with detailed status.
✓Run #4The email to Liam clearly includes '1. JAE-WON KIM HOUSING' as the first section, discussing the housing situation and the unsigned lease.
✓Run #5The email to Liam explicitly mentions Jae-won Kim and his housing situation, stating he is 'still in temporary housing in Austin' with no signed lease.
✓Run #6The email to liam.park@stormcloud.io contains a dedicated section 'JAE-WON KIM — HOUSING / LEASE' discussing Jae-won's housing situation, the Austin apartment, and the temporary housing extension.
3 The email to [liam.park@stormcloud.io](mailto:liam.park@stormcloud.io) states that the guarantor packet was not sent to UrbanNest or that the lease remains unsigned (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Liam states the guarantor packet was promised by 4/24 but only sent 4/26 (i.e., it was late/not done on time) and that the lease countersignature is still awaited — confirming the packet was not sent by the promised deadline and the lease remains unsigned.
✓Run #2The email to Liam explicitly states 'the guarantor packet itself was not sent to UrbanNest until today, April 26' and that Jae-won was still in temporary housing pending the landlord countersign.
✓Run #3The email to Liam states 'The guarantor packet... was sent today, April 26. I told you Thursday it would go out that day. It did not,' clearly admitting the packet was not previously sent and the lease was not yet signed.
✓Run #4The email to Liam explicitly states 'I cannot confirm in our systems that the guarantor packet was actually sent to UrbanNest on Friday...the lease itself is still unsigned. Jae-won appears to still be in temporary housing.'
✓Run #5The email to Liam explicitly states: 'the corporate guarantor packet Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest requested on April 8 was never sent' and acknowledges Jae-won is still in temp housing with no signed lease.
✓Run #6The email to Liam explicitly states 'The guarantor packet was not sent' and that Jae-won is still in temporary housing, making clear the guarantor packet was never delivered to UrbanNest.
4 The email to [liam.park@stormcloud.io](mailto:liam.park@stormcloud.io) addresses the $500 goodwill credit and states that it was posted to a cancelled estimate (EST-2026-0287) instead of the active invoice (INV-2026-0319) (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Liam explicitly states the $500 credit 'was posted to a cancelled estimate' and that a corrected credit memo CR-2026-0319 was created, confirming the misposting on the wrong document was communicated.
✓Run #2The email to Liam states the credit 'was posted against a cancelled internal estimate, not your active invoice' and references INV-2026-002 as the correct invoice. While it doesn't name EST-2026-0287 specifically, it clearly identifies the misposting to a cancelled estimate.
✓Run #3The email to Liam states 'Our finance team confirmed the credit was posted against an internal cancelled estimate rather than your live invoice. That is why it never appeared on your bill.' This correctly identifies the misposting issue.
✓Run #4The email to Liam states 'Marcus Thorne confirmed that the $500 credit was posted against a cancelled internal estimate, not your live invoice (INV-2026-002 for $26,400).' This correctly identifies the misposting (though it references INV-2026-002 rather than INV-2026-0319, it clearly describes the credit being on a cancelled estimate vs. the live invoice).
✓Run #5The email to Liam explicitly addresses the $500 credit, stating 'Marcus Thorne confirmed the credit was posted to a cancelled internal estimate instead of your live invoice (INV-2026-002)' and references EST-2026-0287 in the internal systems.
✓Run #6The email to Liam states 'The $500 was posted against a cancelled internal estimate, not your live invoice' and references moving the credit to 'your active invoice (INV-2026-002)'. This clearly communicates the misposting to a cancelled estimate.
5 The email to [liam.park@stormcloud.io](mailto:liam.park@stormcloud.io) addresses the Simone Richter $340 standing desk reimbursement and states that it is still frozen or held under the blanket stipend review (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Liam addresses Simone Richter's $340 standing desk reimbursement, stating the claim 'was stuck in a blanket fraud hold' — clearly communicating the freeze status.
✓Run #2The email to Liam states 'Simone's reimbursement was caught in a blanket freeze our finance team put in place during an internal review' and that it was 'invisible' to the system, clearly identifying it as frozen/held.
✓Run #3The email to Liam states Simone's claim 'was caught in a platform-wide hold we implemented during an internal review, and it should have been manually released weeks ago.' This correctly identifies the frozen status and cause.
✓Run #4The email to Liam includes section '3. SIMONE RICHTER $340 STANDING DESK' and states it 'was caught in a blanket freeze we put on our expense processing system during an internal review.'
✓Run #5The email to Liam explicitly states Simone Richter's '$340 standing desk reimbursement — STILL FROZEN, BUT TRACKED' and explains it was 'caught in a blanket hold our finance team put on stipend processing during an internal fraud review.'
✓Run #6The email to Liam states 'Simone's claim was frozen under a blanket review hold on our expense system. There was no record of it in our tracking system at all', clearly communicating that the reimbursement is still frozen/blocked.
6 The email to [liam.park@stormcloud.io](mailto:liam.park@stormcloud.io) addresses the Ridgeline Robotics referral and states that David Chen and/or Mina replied to Kenji Watanabe on April 24 (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✗✓✓✓✗ 67%
✓Run #1The email to Liam addresses the Ridgeline Robotics referral and states that 'David Chen and Mina replied 4/24' — directly referencing both responsible parties and the April 24 response date.
✗Run #2The email to Liam states the reply to Kenji was sent TODAY (April 26) by David Chen — not that it was sent on April 24. The agent sent the reply itself rather than finding it had already been sent on April 24. The correct finding per the rubric (that David and Mina replied on April 24) is not reflected.
✓Run #3The email to Liam states 'Both David Chen and I replied to Kenji Watanabe on Thursday, April 24. We apologized for the delay and proposed scheduling an introductory call.' This directly addresses the referral response on April 24.
✓Run #4The email to Liam states under Ridgeline: 'He created CRM records for Ridgeline on Friday and his notes indicate he replied to Kenji' (referring to David on April 24/Friday). The response did not find a sent email to confirm but noted David's claim of replying on April 24.

27 The model must pass a query related to 'credit' or 'goodwill' or 'estimate' or 'StormCloud' (or similar) when calling quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_search_bills to verify the $500 credit posting.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Call #39 (quickbooks_search_invoices) passed criteria with CustomerRef name 'StormCloud' — directly matching the 'StormCloud' keyword criterion.
